title: Pilot jurisdiction selection (scored against explicit criteria) — gates M2–M6
context: Public-Official-Guide must be built against a real jurisdiction to produce valid, primary-source-cited content. The pilot jurisdiction decision is made in M0 and gates M2-M6: it determines the content corpus, source-reuse legality, and the reviewer profile. A dated decision rule is fixed: the jurisdiction must be selected by 2026-08-31. No jurisdiction is currently confirmed; the decision criteria are explicit and scored. The output of this task is the selection record (or a confirmed shortlist with a fixed decision deadline), which becomes a hard dependency for source vetting (009) and all M2 content tasks.
objective: Score candidate jurisdictions against the four explicit selection criteria and record the chosen jurisdiction (or a confirmed shortlist with the 2026-08-31 decision deadline), with rationale, so M2-M6 content and source-reuse verification can proceed against the right corpus and reviewer profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Scores candidate jurisdictions against all four explicit criteria: (1) statutes/regulations reusable under the edict-of-government doctrine with no copyrighted compiled-code/annotation lock-in; (2) high density of small, under-resourced offices; (3) an available credentialed reviewer (ethics professional / licensed attorney) for that jurisdiction; (4) manageable ethics-code complexity
Records the chosen jurisdiction or a scored shortlist plus the fixed decision deadline of 2026-08-31 with rationale for each criterion
The decision record becomes the dependency that unblocks public-official-guide-sources-009 and all M2 content tasks
Reviewed by Maintainer and flagged for Expert (ethics/legal) confirmation once a candidate is selected
output: A scored jurisdiction-selection document recording the chosen pilot jurisdiction (or confirmed shortlist + decision deadline) with rationale against the four explicit criteria, suitable for use as the dependency anchor for M2-M6.
